title: phalangium revolutum
side_effects: Phalangium revolutum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues. Ingesting the plant's leaves or stems can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure to its sap may cause skin lesions and blistering. Improper use of the plant can also trigger respiratory problems, including asthma-like symptoms and coughing, especially in individuals with pre-existing conditions. Furthermore, excessive consumption of the plant's extracts may lead to kidney damage and liver toxicity, highlighting the importance of proper handling and dosage.
